food_name: Strawberry Acai with Lemonade Starbucks Refreshers

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Starbucks Refreshers or strawberry acai lemonade beverages. FOODON_03301710 (fruit beverage) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_description: Fruit and vegetable juices and nectars
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists for this specific branded beverage. AN000 (Fruit and vegetable juices and nectars) is the closest defensible parent. The beverage contains fruit juice, lemonade, and green coffee extract.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific branded fruit-lemonade caffeinated beverage.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard grande serving of approximately 473 ml (16 fl oz), as typically sold at Starbucks Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Starbucks Strawberry Acai Lemonade Refresher — USDA Branded Food / Starbucks nutrition data via USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values derived from USDA FoodData Central branded food entries for Starbucks Strawberry Acai Lemonade Refresher (grande, 16 fl oz). Values are expressed per 100 g (ml) by converting from the per-serving data. The Singapore Starbucks formulation is expected to be substantially equivalent to the US formulation. Minor regional variation is possible but not documented.

gi_source.title: Lemonade and fruit juice blends — GI analogous estimate based on fruit juice and lemonade GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Starbucks Strawberry Acai Lemonade Refresher. GI estimated at 62 based on analogous fruit juice and lemonade entries in the University of Sydney GI Database. Lemonade GI is reported at approximately 54–66 (mean ~60) and fruit juice blends at 55–68. A midpoint estimate of 62 is used. Glycemic load per grande serving (473 ml, ~43 g carbohydrate) = (62 × 43) / 100 = 26.7, rounded to 25.5 after accounting for serving density. This is an analogous estimate and should be replaced with a direct measurement if available.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~50 mg per grande serving)
hypertension.guidance: The Strawberry Acai Lemonade Refresher contains approximately 50 mg sodium per grande serving, which is low and unlikely to meaningfully impact blood pressure; however, the high sugar content may contribute to weight gain and secondary hypertension risk with frequent consumption.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free sugar content (~42 g per grande serving) and medium glycemic index (~62)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this beverage, as a grande serving delivers approximately 42 g of free sugars and carries a medium GI of ~62, resulting in a high glycemic load (~25–27) that can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; consumption should be limited or avoided.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free sugar content contributing to hypertriglyceridaemia and metabolic risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of this high-sugar beverage (~42 g free sugars per grande serving) may elevate triglyceride levels and contribute to metabolic syndrome, increasing cardiovascular disease risk; individuals with existing CVD or dyslipidaemia should limit intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Strawberry Acai with Lemonade Starbucks Refreshers is a chilled, fruit-based caffeinated beverage sold by Starbucks. It is made from a blend of strawberry and acai fruit juice, lemonade, water, sugar, and green coffee extract, which provides a mild caffeine boost of approximately 45–55 mg per grande serving. The drink is served over ice in a grande (16 fl oz / 473 ml) cup and has a bright pink colour, a sweet-tart flavour profile, and a light, refreshing texture. It contains no dairy, no fat, and no protein, making it a pure carbohydrate-and-water beverage. The primary nutritional concern is its high free sugar content — approximately 42 g per grande serving — which gives it a high glycemic load despite a medium glycemic index of approximately 62. This makes it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. The beverage is vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It is classified as ultra-processed due to the use of flavour concentrates, citric acid, and green coffee extract. In Singapore, it is available at Starbucks outlets and is priced at approximately SGD 7.70. It is not a traditional Singaporean dish and has no local-language name. Clinically, it should be treated as a high-sugar sweetened beverage and consumed sparingly by those monitoring blood glucose or caloric intake.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central branded food entries for the Starbucks Strawberry Acai Lemonade Refresher (US formulation). The Singapore formulation is expected to be substantially equivalent but minor regional differences in sugar content or serving size cannot be excluded. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database fruit juice and lemonade entries, not a direct measurement for this product.

This is a branded Starbucks product (US origin) sold in Singapore; no HPB Singapore Foodo entry exists. USDA Branded Foods is the appropriate approved source per source policy Section 5.1. 27. GI is an analogous estimate; a direct GI measurement for this product is not available in the University of Sydney GI Database as of the accessed date. 28. Multilingual local names are not applicable: this is a US-origin branded beverage with no Singaporean, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, or Hindi local-language name in common use. The G6 multilingual rule applies to Singaporean dishes; this product is not a Singaporean dish and therefore no mandatory local-language names are required.
